text: When plans change suddenly at the last minute, what happens in your body?

opts.t: I flex easily — change is just weather.
A wobble, then I adapt.
A spike of panic I have to actively manage.
It can unravel my whole day.

text: When your security is threatened, your first move is…

opts.t: Calm assessment, then decisive action.
Make a plan — maybe over-plan.
Take control of everything and everyone.
Anger and blame — someone did this to me.

text: How easily do you release possessions, homes, or routines that no longer serve you?

opts.t: Gracefully — I trust what comes next.
Slowly, but I do let go.
I hold on long past the expiry date.
I cannot let go — the holding IS my safety.

text: How often do emotions arrive faster and bigger than you can process?

opts.t: Rarely — feelings move through at a livable pace.
Sometimes, in stressful seasons.
Often — I flood, then need days to recover.
Constantly — I feel hostage to my own weather.

text: Has pleasure quietly become another performance — something to optimize or win?

opts.t: No — I can savor without measuring.
A little; I catch myself rating experiences.
Yes — even vacations have goals.
Completely — I cannot enjoy without achieving.

text: Is your comfort actually nourishment — or has it become a soft numbing?

opts.t: True nourishment — I rest and re-emerge alive.
Mostly real, with some cozy avoidance.
Half-numbing — the couch is a hiding place.
Numbing — comfort is how I avoid feeling at all.

text: After you make a decision, what happens next?

opts.t: I stand in it and move forward.
A brief review, then commitment.
Second-guessing loops that drain me.
I often reverse it — or let others decide for me.

text: What does being publicly wrong feel like?

opts.t: Information. I update and move on.
A sting I metabolize fairly quickly.
A threat — I defend even when I doubt myself.
Unbearable — I will win the argument at any cost.

text: When something in your life clearly needs to change, how long before you act?

opts.t: Soon — I move once I see clearly.
A while — I need to warm up to action.
Months or years — I wait to be forced.
I mostly don't — inertia decides for me.

text: In close relationships, how loud is the fear of being left?

opts.t: Quiet — I feel secure even in distance.
Present sometimes, but I can soothe it.
Loud — I scan for signs of leaving.
Deafening — I leave first, or cling hard.

text: Do you keep score in love — of efforts, wrongs, who apologized last?

opts.t: No — love isn't a ledger for me.
Occasionally, when I feel unseen.
Yes — the tally runs in the background.
Always — and the debt is never repaid.

text: When a relationship or attachment has truly ended, can you release it?

opts.t: Yes — with grief, gratitude, and time.
Eventually, though I carry it a while.
Barely — old loves still occupy the house.
No — I hold on for years, even in pain.

text: When you're nervous, what happens to your speech?

opts.t: It stays steady — nerves don't take my voice.
A little fast, still coherent.
I over-talk, scatter, fill every silence.
I either flood with words or lose them completely.

text: In conflict, what do your words become?

opts.t: Clear and clean — firm without wounding.
Sharper than intended, then repaired.
Blades — I know exactly where to cut.
Weapons I regret — or icy silence as punishment.

text: How often do you swallow your truth to keep the peace?

opts.t: Rarely — peace built on my silence isn't peace.
Sometimes, for small things.
Often — my real opinions stay underground.
Almost always — no one knows what I actually think.

text: What is the speed of your thoughts, honestly?

opts.t: Spacious — thoughts arise, serve, and settle.
Quick but steerable.
Racing — tabs open everywhere, hard to land.
A storm I can't slow, especially at night.

text: How do you hold your own opinions?

opts.t: Lightly — strong views, loosely held.
Firmly, but genuinely revisable.
Rigidly — being right feels like survival.
As identity — changing my mind feels like dying.

text: Is there a truth about your life you keep choosing not to look at?

opts.t: No — I look, even when it costs me.
One or two I circle slowly.
Yes — the fog around it is half-chosen.
Yes — and I keep myself busy so I never have to.

text: What does your spiritual seeking look like?

opts.t: A steady, rooted practice that deepens.
A main path with curious detours.
Chasing every new practice, mastering none.
Frantic seeking that never lets me arrive.

text: Be honest: is your spirituality partly an achievement?

opts.t: No — it's a surrender, not a resume.
A little pride sneaks in sometimes.
Yes — I track my progress toward "awakened."
Entirely — I need to be the most conscious person in the room.

text: When did you last feel genuine awe — the sacred, unmistakably present?

opts.t: Recently — awe visits me often.
Within the year; I can still be pierced.
A long time ago — the channel feels dusty.
I can't remember — nothing feels sacred anymore.
